_description: Tier 1 scenario family weights. Each trade activates at most one market-state context (first match in priority order). A second context will be added later from user preferences (Tier 2), and the two weight vectors will be blended. For now, one context fires and its adjustments are applied directly.
_selection: first_match — contexts are evaluated top-to-bottom; the first one whose conditions are all satisfied is applied. Order matters: more specific conditions should come before broader ones.
_what_the_weights_mean: Each weight expresses our prior belief about how much a scenario family contributes to the expected P&L of this trade type. Weights are NOT structure-dependent — the same vector is applied to every shortlisted structure so scores are directly comparable.
_baseline_logic: All 8 families start at 1/8 = 0.125 (equal-weighted, no view). The active context adjusts individual families up or down. After adjustments, weights are floored (no family below `floor`) and renormalised to sum to 1. If no context fires, the baseline equal-weight vector is used unchanged.

_context_schema.id: Stable identifier — used to display the active context in the UI and in version history.
_context_schema.comment: Plain-English description of the trade archetype this context represents and why the weights are set as they are. This is the reasoning a PM reads when sense-checking.
_context_schema.when: Array of {field, op, value} — ALL must be true for this context to be selected. Supported fields: target_z_abs, carry_regime, with_carry, T, vol, atmfsratio. Supported ops: '>', '>=', '<', '<=', '==', '!='. If a field is None on the MarketState (e.g. target_z when no target supplied), the condition evaluates false.
_context_schema.adjustments: Object mapping family → signed delta. All families listed here are adjusted when this context is selected. Positive = this scenario matters more for this trade type, negative = less.

contexts.comment: Tenor under 1 month: theta and pin risk dominate everything else. The terminal intrinsic value (EXPIRY) is what the PM should focus on — there is almost no time for path scenarios to develop independently from the final outcome. Early-touch scenarios are near-equivalent to expiry at this horizon and should be de-emphasised to avoid double-counting.

High carry (regime 2) trading against the carry: the carry is a structural, continuous headwind. Every day the trade is open, theta and the forward drift both work against the position. The dominant risk is an adverse drift (WRONG_WAY) and a flat market that bleeds premium (NO_MOVE). Getting to the target requires the market to fight its own structural tendency — CORRECT_PATH is the hardest outcome to achieve.

High carry, trading with the carry, targeting a distant level (>1.5σ from forward). The drift is your ally and the target requires a sustained, conviction-driven move. When these moves happen they often overshoot. The dominant paths are a smooth carry-driven advance (CORRECT_PATH) and an extension beyond the target (OVERSHOOT). Wrong-way moves are fighting both the drift and the market's natural tendency.

High carry, trading with the carry, targeting a level close to the forward (<0.5σ). This is essentially a carry-capture trade — the target is so close that normal market noise will touch it. The main risk is an early touch followed by a reversal before expiry (EARLY_TARGET). A sustained correct-path move is also likely. Overshoot is less meaningful at this distance.

High carry, trading with the carry, moderate target distance. The drift is working in our favour. A smooth progression toward the target is the most likely path. Wrong-way moves require the market to work against both the direction of the trade and the carry, which is a double headwind for the adverse scenario.

Noisy carry (regime 0) with elevated vol (>20%): there is no reliable directional drift signal, and vol moves are large enough to materially affect option value independent of direction. The dominant driver of P&L is the vol surface, not the forward path. VOL_SENSITIVITY scenarios deserve heavy weight. A flat market (NO_MOVE) is less meaningful without carry pressure.

Noisy carry (regime 0): the carry signal is weak relative to vol. The trade is a pure directional bet without a structural drift to support it. Vol-driven mean reversion is a larger factor than in high-carry regimes. NO_MOVE is less meaningful (there is no carry bleed), and VOL_SENSITIVITY deserves more weight.

Tenor over 6 months: there is a large window for the market to reach the target early and then drift away before expiry — a real risk for capped structures and spreads. EARLY_TARGET (touch-and-retrace) deserves elevated weight. Vol sensitivity also matters more because vol moves have more time to compound.

Target is distant from the forward (>1.5σ) with moderate carry. Getting there requires a sustained move with conviction — which tends to overshoot. Early touches are unlikely at this distance. This applies when carry is not the dominant factor (carry_regime 0 or 1) so the signal is purely about the target distance.

Target is close to the forward (<0.5σ) with moderate carry. The level is so easy to reach that random noise will touch it. The dominant risk for capped structures is an early touch followed by a pull-back — the trade gets triggered but then reverses. Overshooting a level this close is less informative.

ATM vol above 20% with moderate carry (regime 1). Large vol moves are a meaningful source of P&L variation beyond directional outcomes. Vega-rich structures benefit; short-vol structures are exposed. Elevate VOL_SENSITIVITY to reflect this.
